fabricated_divergences.quote: German-language coverage (Tagesschau) and English-language coverage (DW News, France 24) both address Budapest but diverge in framing Péter Magyar's position: DW and Tagesschau report he pledged not to continue the ban, whereas France 24's correspondent emphasizes his ambiguity, absence from the march, and avoidance of the issue during the campaign.
fabricated_divergences.why_unsupported: The DW analysis characterizes Magyar's stance as 'cautious, non-committal' and notes he is 'not yet reversing Orban-era restrictions' — i.e., DW's framing aligns with France 24's emphasis on ambiguity, not with a clear pledge. Only Tagesschau's analysis reports Magyar 'pledged not to continue the ban.' Grouping DW alongside Tagesschau against France 24 misattributes DW's framing and manufactures a contrast the analyses do not support. It is also not a clean cross-language-group divergence since DW and France 24 are both English-language.
